requirements.summary: Enforce that only the username 'TailsTheBeast124' can access the application after Internet Identity login
acceptanceCriteria: After logging in via Internet Identity, if the resolved username is not 'TailsTheBeast124', the AccessDeniedScreen is displayed and no app routes are accessible.
When logged in as 'TailsTheBeast124', the app loads normally with full access.
The AccessDeniedScreen includes a logout button that clears the Internet Identity session.
No other username — even a valid registered account — can access any page of the app.

file_operations.description: Add username restriction logic after authentication and profile fetch. After the user profile is successfully loaded via getCallerUserProfile, check if profile.name equals 'TailsTheBeast124'. If not, set a new state flag (e.g., isAccessDenied) to true and render the AccessDeniedScreen component instead of the onboarding flow or children. Ensure this check occurs after onboarding is complete and before rendering the main app content. The check must apply regardless of approval status, account lock state, or other conditions.

Update the AccessDeniedScreen component to accept an onLogout callback prop. Ensure the logout button calls this callback to trigger the Internet Identity logout flow (via clear() from useInternetIdentity) and clears all cached React Query data. Update the message text to clearly state that only the user 'TailsTheBeast124' is permitted to access this application.
